hypothesis: Using ML surrogates (eqV2 or eSEN) to generate an approximate minimum energy path and restarting VASP-based SSNEB from those ML-converged images will reduce SSNEB iterations and computational time to converge the MEP for CsPbI3 and TiO2 transformations, while yielding transition states and minimum-energy paths with accuracy comparable to conventional VASP-only SSNEB.
